name: Buddy Bolden
abstract: Charles Joseph "Buddy" Bolden (September 6, 1877 – November 4, 1931) was an American cornetist who was regarded by contemporaries and later jazz scholars as a key figure in the development of a New Orleans style of ragtime music, or "jass", which later came to be known as jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Betty Lee Hanson, Joe James, George Lewis, Joe Lewis, Joe Butler, Kid Thomas Valentine, Bill Russell, Allan Jaffe, Sandra Jaffe
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee lists names but does not speak about Buddy Bolden. The mention of Buddy Bolden occurs entirely within the [INTERVIEWER]'s turn, where the interviewer recounts stories told by others (Kid Ori, Bud Scott, Mutt Carey) about Bolden's church background.
about_quotes.doc_title: Betty Lee Hanson, Joe James, George Lewis, Joe Lewis, Joe Butler, Kid Thomas Valentine, Bill Russell, Allan Jaffe, Sandra Jaffe Interview, 1961-11-25

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: one of things about jazz, at least for me, and just from the little history that I’ve read about certain performers and the origins in the beginnings of jazz music, one thing that might be different in some sort of way about the early musicians, like Buddy Bolden
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: we still don't know much about Buddy Bolden, for instance... I remember one time speaking of Sam... about maybe as long as three years ago... on Bolden by the New York people... are just going to cross him off the list as really never having existed.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (William Russell) is narrating what Norwood Williams told him: 'Williams was enthusiastic about Buddy Bolden remembering the Take It Away... He said he had played with Bolden's... He had been a member of Bolden's Troubadours'

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses a movie about Buddy Bolden and compares it to 'Young Man With a Horn', indicating knowledge of the historical figure through media and cultural associations, but no personal interaction is described.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: I've done a little research as well. I've done some technical work on his so-called recordings... there is no sign of any records of Buddy Bolden... his prowess, if any, must remain a beautiful legend, a fairy story
